nameMeanings.name: Rockhill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Middlesex and Surrey): perhaps a habitational name from any of several minor places called Rockhill, such as Rockhill Farm in Stourton Caundle (Dorset) or Rockhill Farm in Greete (Shropshire). However, early forms for these particular places have not been found and so the surname may alternatively be a variant of   Rochelle   (compare   Rockett  ).

variants.name: Rochelle
meanings: French (mainly Ille-et-Vilaine) and English (Staffordshire; of Norman origin): habitational name from (La) Rochelle, the name of several places in various parts of France, especially that in Manche, from a diminutive of Old French roche ‘rock, cliff, promontory’. In Britain, the surname is often recorded in its Norman French form Rokel(l)e.

nameMeanings.name: GODfrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Old French name Godefroy, which is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from god ‘god’ (or gōd ‘good’) + fred, frid ‘peace’. This was adopted by the Normans, and brought to England by immigrants from the Low Countries and France. It was borne by a Norman saint ( c.1066–1115 ) who became bishop of Amiens. There has been considerable confusion with Geoffrey .

en.lifeSummary: GODfrey Rockhill was born in 1070, in Rock Hill, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in Arkesden, Essex, England, United Kingdom.
